talk_metadata.talk_title: Gut Microeukaryotes, Endogenous Viruses, and Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "At the very least, it may indicate that it has a non‑pathogenic role given how prevalent it is"
Speaker said: "We think that this may have implications for primary and secondary prevention of obesity‑mediated cancers"

key_data_gaps: No mechanistic validation of Blastocystis effects
No longitudinal intervention data beyond short‑term diet trial

crispro_opportunity.description: Blastocystis prevalence correlates with plant‑based diet, favorable cardiometabolic markers, and lower colorectal cancer odds, suggesting a potential non‑invasive biomarker for cancer risk stratification.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Blastocystis prevalence was positively associated with healthy diets and negatively associated with colorectal cancer"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics and microbial sequencing to develop a predictive model linking Blastocystis subtypes to cancer risk, enabling patient‑level stratification.

crispro_opportunity.description: The randomized dietary intervention showed modest Blastocystis expansion only in weight‑loss achievers; a more controlled trial could test causality for cancer prevention.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "In a randomized controlled trial of 1,100 individuals, blastocystis prevalence could expand modestly, particularly among people who were adhering to a higher quality diet"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial power and identify optimal enrollment criteria (e.g., baseline Blastocystis subtype, diet score) to avoid dilution of signal.

unresolved_questions: What are the mechanistic pathways by which Blastocystis influences cardiometabolic biomarkers?
Can specific Blastocystis subtypes be causally linked to reduced colorectal cancer incidence?
How does trans‑kingdom dysbiosis in MASLD translate to therapeutic targets?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Population‑scale EBV persistence and disease risk
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are essentially identifying a segment of the population that's in this right‑hand tail, that they have very high levels of persistent EBV"
Speaker said: "These 10% of people are special in the way that they processed or essentially failed to process the virus"

key_data_gaps: Limited functional validation of identified host loci
No prospective interventional studies targeting EBV persistence

crispro_opportunity.description: Genetic loci linked to EBV persistence (e.g., antigen presentation genes) could be targeted to enhance viral clearance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We detected 25 conditionally independent loci... many genes that are reassuring that they probably have something to do with either B cells or viruses"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize druggable targets within the identified pathways and model their impact on EBV DNA load.

crispro_opportunity.description: High‑EBV‑DNA individuals constitute a ~10% high‑risk subgroup for autoimmune and neoplastic diseases; a biomarker assay could stratify patients for surveillance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "These 10% of individuals have very high levels of persistent EBV... and are more likely to develop lung cancer, lymphomas, autoimmune diseases"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate EBV DNA quantification with host genotype to generate a risk score for longitudinal monitoring.

crispro_opportunity.description: The described whole‑genome‑sequencing‑derived EBV detection platform can be extended to ~100 other viral species, offering a broader virome surveillance capability.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We focused on EBV, given quite a bit of prior work, but this is one of probably 100 viral species that we've been able to characterize"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s computational spine can be adapted to detect low‑abundance viral DNA across biobanks, outperforming conventional serology.

unresolved_questions: Which of the 45 identified loci are causally involved in EBV latency versus clearance?
Do specific EBV strain variants modulate persistence risk beyond host genetics?
Can therapeutic reduction of EBV DNA load lower incidence of associated cancers?

talk_metadata.talk_title: The cancer mycobiome: truth, myth, and current data
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Candida albicans is mucosal‑associated and drives IL‑17/IL‑1 responses that can be genotoxic via candidalysin"
Speaker said: "Fluconazole cleared Candida from both mouth and gut, whereas oral nystatin only cleared the mouth"

key_data_gaps: Small patient numbers (n≈53) for antifungal intervention
Limited mechanistic data linking candidalysin to tumor progression in humans

crispro_opportunity.description: Systemic fluconazole reduces gut Candida and restores beneficial butyrate‑producing bacteria, suggesting a combination of antifungal and microbiome‑modulating therapy for colorectal cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Fluconazole cleared Candida from the gut and led to recovery of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii and Roseburia intestinalis"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of antifungal agents with probiotic or FMT strategies to predict tumor‑microbiome response.

crispro_opportunity.description: Strain‑level variation in Candida albicans (candidalysin production, hyphal formation) may predict which patients benefit from antifungal therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We observed phenotypical differences among Candida albicans strains; some produce hyphae and candidalysin, others do not"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can incorporate strain‑specific genomic signatures to stratify patients for targeted antifungal trials.

crispro_opportunity.description: Future trials should include longitudinal metagenomic and metabolomic endpoints to avoid dilution of signal seen in the current 8‑week pilot.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We only looked at induction; longer follow‑up and more comprehensive omics would clarify ecological effects"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate power calculations for multi‑omics endpoints and recommend optimal sampling intervals.

unresolved_questions: Which Candida albicans strain features (e.g., candidalysin expression) most strongly drive tumor‑promoting inflammation?
Can antifungal‑induced restoration of butyrate‑producing bacteria translate into measurable clinical benefit in CRC patients?
What is the relative contribution of fungal versus bacterial dysbiosis across different tumor types?
